input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS → iOS + Android.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

**Source:** [https://dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download)

- Download the latest **.NET 8 SDK** (or .NET 9 if you want the latest preview/stable).
- Use the macOS **Arm64** installer if on Apple Silicon, **x64** for Intel.
- Verify: `dotnet --list-sdks`

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, `maui-maccatalyst`, and dependencies.
- Discover available workloads: `dotnet workload search`
- Verify: `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` as installed.

## 3. Xcode (for iOS)

**Source:** Mac App Store or [https://developer.apple.com/xcode/](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)

- Install the **latest stable Xcode** (Xcode 15+ for .NET 8, Xcode 16+ for .NET 9).
- After install, run:
  ```bash
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  ```
- Accept the license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- Install iOS simulators: **Xcode → Settings → Platforms → iOS**.
- Verify: `xcodebuild -version` and `xcrun simctl list devices`

## 4. Android SDK + JDK (for Android)

**Recommended approach — let .NET handle it:**

```bash
dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net8.0-android \
  -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" \
  -p:JavaSdkDirectory="$HOME/.android-jdk" \
  -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
```

Run this against any MAUI project to auto-provision the Android SDK, NDK, build-tools, platform, and emulator images.

**Manual alternative (if you prefer explicit control):**

| Component | Source |
|---|---|
| **JDK 17** | [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) |
| **Android Studio** | [https://developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) (use SDK Manager inside) |

Set environment variables in `~/.zshrc`:
```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
export JAVA_HOME="/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/microsoft-17.jdk/Contents/Home"
export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$ANDROID_HOME/emulator:$PATH"
```

**Required SDK components** (via `sdkmanager` or Android Studio):
- `platform-tools`, `build-tools;34.0.0`, `platforms;android-34`, `emulator`, `system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a` (Apple Silicon) or `x86_64` (Intel).

Verify: `dotnet build -f net8.0-android` succeeds on a template project.

## 5. IDE (pick one)

| IDE | Source | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code + C# Dev Kit** | [https://code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) + `.NET MAUI` extension | Lightweight, free |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** | **Discontinued** — do not install | |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) | Full-featured, paid |

## 6. Create & Verify a Test Project

```bash
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp

# Build for all targets
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios
dotnet build -f net8.0-android
dotnet build -f net8.0-maccatalyst

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
emulator -avd Pixel_API_34 &
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run

# Run on Mac (native)
dotnet run -f net8.0-maccatalyst
```

## 7. Version Discovery Cheat Sheet

| Question | Command |
|---|---|
| Which .NET SDKs are installed? | `dotnet --list-sdks` |
| Which workloads? | `dotnet workload list` |
| Latest workload versions available? | `dotnet workload update --dry-run` |
| Xcode version? | `xcodebuild -version` |
| Android SDK components? | `$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --list` |
| JDK version? | `java -version` |
| Supported TFMs for your SDK? | Check [release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) |

## Key Gotchas

- **Apple Silicon:** Use Arm64 installers everywhere. Android emulator needs `arm64-v8a` system images.
- **Xcode CLT alone is not enough** — full Xcode.app is required for iOS simulator builds.
- **Free Apple ID** works for simulator-only dev. Physical device deployment requires an [Apple Developer account](https://developer.apple.com/programs/) ($99/year).
- Run `dotnet workload update` periodically to keep MAUI patches current.
